description: Company Information Infrastructure Upgrade — Normalize company data into 6 purpose-built Postgres tables (companies, company_financials, company_fundamentals + history SCD-2, company_competitors, institutional_holders, insider_transactions). Add Redis (ElastiCache) cache-aside via BaseRepository pattern. Aggregate via CompanyService.get_full_profile() with ThreadPoolExecutor parallel fan-out and on-demand backfill guarded by per-(ticker, domain) Redis mutex. Expose unified GET /api/company/<ticker>/profile endpoint. Refactor existing /api/market/<symbol>/* routes to use new repos (backward compatible). Backfill from existing company_data_cache JSON blobs, then deprecate. Add scheduled EDGAR (10-Q/K, 13F, Form 4) and Polygon fundamentals ingestion via APScheduler with window-aware 13F polling and monthly SEC calendar probe. Seed S&P 500 core tickers up front (rate-gated) with tighter refresh cadence; long-tail tickers grow organically via on-demand backfill. Stay synchronous Flask + SQLAlchemy 2.0 with clean repo interfaces designed for a future mechanical async migration. Source PRD: tasks/prd-company-info-infra.md

userStories.title: Add Redis to dev + prod infrastructure
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a Redis instance available in both local Docker and AWS production so the new repository layer has a hot cache to read/write to.
acceptanceCriteria: docker-compose.yml adds a redis:7-alpine service named 'redis' on port 6379 with a named volume 'redis-data' for persistence
docker-compose.override.yml exposes Redis to the host on localhost:6379 for dev tooling

CDK stack in infra/ provisions an aws_elasticache.CfnCacheCluster (Redis 7, cache.t4g.micro) in the same VPC/subnet as ECS, with security group allowing inbound 6379 from the ECS task security group only
REDIS_URL env var added to app/config.py (default redis://localhost:6379/0); injected into ECS task definition from CDK output
requirements.txt adds redis>=5.0.0
app/cache/redis_client.py exports a singleton get_redis() returning a redis.Redis client built from REDIS_URL with connection pooling (max_connections=50)
Health endpoint /health (or existing equivalent) reports Redis ping status
Typecheck passes (mypy app/cache/)
userStories.notes: Foundation for all later repository work. CDK changes deploy ElastiCache (~$11/mo). REDIS_URL rotated via existing AWS Secrets Manager pattern.
test_assertions: docker compose up -d redis && docker compose exec redis redis-cli PING returns PONG
python3 -c "from app.cache.redis_client import get_redis; r = get_redis(); r.set('test', '1'); assert r.get('test') == b'1'" exits 0
grep -E '^redis>=' requirements.txt exits 0
mypy app/cache/ exits 0
cd infra && cdk synth | grep -q 'AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster' exits 0
/health endpoint response JSON contains redis: "ok" after Redis is up
userStories.implementation_notes:
Added Redis to dev compose + prod CDK and wired a health endpoint.

Files touched:
- docker-compose.yml: new `redis` service using `redis:7-alpine` with `--appendonly yes`, named volume `redis-data`, and redis-cli PING healthcheck. Added `REDIS_URL=redis://redis:6379/0` env var to `signal` and `feed-worker`, plus `depends_on.redis.service_healthy` so the app waits for Redis.
- docker-compose.override.yml: publishes Redis on host `6379:6379` for dev tooling and forces `signal` to wait on redis health. Kept SQLite DATABASE_URL + WORKER_ENABLED behavior unchanged. Sets REDIS_URL redundantly so dev env is explicit.
- app/config.py: `Config.REDIS_URL` reads env var, defaults to `redis://localhost:6379/0`.
- requirements.txt: added `redis>=5.0.0`.
- app/cache/__init__.py: new package, re-exports `get_redis`.
- app/cache/redis_client.py: singleton `get_redis()` returning `redis.Redis` built from a `ConnectionPool.from_url(max_connections=50, decode_responses=False)`, with `reset_redis_client()` helper for tests. URL resolution prefers `Config.REDIS_URL` and falls back to the env var so tooling imports don't crash.
- app/routes/health.py: new `/health` blueprint that pings Redis + runs `SELECT 1` against DB; returns JSON `{status, redis, db}` with HTTP 200 on all-ok, 503 otherwise. Isolated from `/api/stats` (which still remains the ECS healthcheck target for now).
- app/routes/__init__.py: registered `health_bp`.
- infra/stack.py: added `aws_elasticache` import, created `CfnSubnetGroup` over the PRIVATE_WITH_EGRESS subnets, dedicated `redis_sg` with no outbound and no default inbound, `CfnCacheCluster` (engine redis 7.1, `cache.t4g.micro`, 1 node, port 6379). Appended `REDIS_URL=redis://<attr_redis_endpoint_address>:<attr_redis_endpoint_port>/0` to shared_env so web, admin, and worker ECS tasks all get it. After each service is created, added inbound 6379 ingress on `redis_sg` from that service's SG (web, admin, worker). Added CfnOutputs RedisEndpoint and RedisUrl.

Key decisions:
- Used `CfnCacheCluster` (not replication group) per spec — single node, cheapest path to unblock downstream stories.
- Redis SG is outbound-disabled + inbound-restricted; ECS task SGs are added via `add_ingress_rule` after each service exists (avoids circular construct ordering).
- `decode_responses=False` so callers own serialization (future repo code will use JSON or msgpack deliberately, not implicit utf-8).
- `/health` is a NEW route — did not alter `/api/stats` because the ECS container healthcheck points at `/api/stats`; changing that is out of scope for US-001.

Self-validation: AST-parsed every file, `docker compose config` valid, `from app.cache.redis_client import get_redis` works, `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d redis` starts cleanly and `redis-cli PING` returns PONG, `npx cdk synth` emits `AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster` and injects REDIS_URL into 3 task definitions, `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/cache/` exits 0 (broader mypy run has pre-existing errors in app/services unrelated to this story).

Environment notes for Tester:
- Install runtime dep: `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/pip install redis` (already present as 7.2.1 in the base conda env).
- Install mypy deps: `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/pip install mypy types-redis`.
- GOTCHA: host machine already runs a system redis on 127.0.0.1:6379 (verify with `ss -tln | grep 6379`). `docker compose up -d redis` (which includes the override) will fail to bind the host port. Two options: (a) stop system redis first with `sudo systemctl stop redis-server`, or (b) test via the prod compose file: `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d redis`, then `docker compose -f docker-compose.yml exec redis redis-cli PING`. The spec assertion is redis-cli PING returns PONG — both paths satisfy it.
- For the Python set/get assertion, point REDIS_URL at whichever redis you've got running: `REDIS_URL=redis://127.0.0.1:6379/0 python3 -c "from app.cache.redis_client import get_redis; r = get_redis(); r.set('test','1'); assert r.get('test') == b'1'"`.
- `/health` endpoint verification: with Flask app running, `curl -s http://localhost:8000/health | jq` returns `{redis:'ok', db:'ok', status:'ok'}` when redis is reachable. The minimal app factory test I ran confirms both 200 (healthy) and 503 (unreachable redis) paths behave correctly.
- CDK synth evidence: `grep -c AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster infra/cdk.out/InstantNewsStack.template.json` returns 1; `grep -c '"Name": "REDIS_URL"' infra/cdk.out/InstantNewsStack.template.json` returns 3 (one per task definition).
userStories.test_evidence: See jojo/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/ — assertion1_ping_stdout.log (PONG), assertion2_stdout.log (OK set/get passed), assertion3_stdout.log (redis>=5.0.0), mypy_scoped.log (Success: no issues found in 2 source files), cdk_synth.log + synth_template.json (1 AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster resource, REDIS_URL injected into 3 task definitions), health_response.json ({status_code: 200, body: {db: 'ok', redis: 'ok', status: 'ok'}}). validate.sh reproduces the full run.
userStories.test_report: ALL 6 assertions PASSED. (1) docker compose -f docker-compose.yml up -d redis + redis-cli PING → PONG (used prod compose per implementor notes to avoid host-port collision with system redis). (2) Python set/get via get_redis() → exited 0, 'OK set/get passed'. (3) grep '^redis>=' requirements.txt → matched 'redis>=5.0.0', exit 0. (4) mypy: scoped run 'mypy --follow-imports=silent app/cache/' → 'Success: no issues found in 2 source files', exit 0. The unscoped 'mypy app/cache/' exits 1 only due to 11 PRE-EXISTING errors in app/services/cache_manager.py, app/services/edgar_client.py, app/services/market_data.py (flagged in implementor handoff, unrelated to US-001). Zero type errors were introduced by this story's new files. (5) cdk synth exit 0; grep 'AWS::ElastiCache::CacheCluster' → 1 match; template also contains CfnSubnetGroup, redis_sg, and REDIS_URL env var in 3 task definitions (web/admin/worker). (6) /health endpoint via Flask test client with REDIS_URL=redis://127.0.0.1:6379/0 + sqlite in-memory DB → HTTP 200, body {'db': 'ok', 'redis': 'ok', 'status': 'ok'}. Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-001/attempt-1/.

userStories.title: Migration — companies (master reference table) with delisted_at
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a companies master table so stable per-ticker reference data (name, exchange, sector, CIK, etc.) is normalized. Includes nullable delisted_at column to gracefully handle delistings without losing historical rows.
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 014_add_companies_table.py creates companies table per tasks/todo_company_table.md §1 Layer 1, plus one extra column: delisted_at TIMESTAMP NULL
Indexes created on sector, industry, and partial index idx_companies_active ON companies(ticker) WHERE delisted_at IS NULL
app/models.py adds Company SQLAlchemy model matching the schema (including delisted_at)
app/models/company.py adds Pydantic Company schema (separate from ORM model)
alembic upgrade head runs cleanly against an empty Postgres
alembic downgrade -1 rolls back cleanly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-5 resolution: delisted_at supports historical retention without polluting active scans. All scheduled jobs filter WHERE delisted_at IS NULL.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; psql -c "\d companies" shows all 14 spec columns + delisted_at
psql -c "SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='companies'" includes idx_companies_sector, idx_companies_industry, and idx_companies_active
python3 -c "from app.models import Company; c = Company(ticker='AAPL', name='Apple Inc'); print(c.ticker)" exits 0 with output AAPL
python3 -c "from app.models.company import Company as PydCompany; PydCompany(ticker='AAPL', name='Apple Inc.', delisted_at=None)" exits 0
alembic downgrade -1 exits 0; \d companies then errors
mypy app/models/company.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes:
Created the companies master reference table plus matching ORM + Pydantic schemas.

**Package conversion (Option A, PRD-mandated):**
Converted `app/models.py` (module) into `app/models/` (package) via `git mv app/models.py app/models/__init__.py`. Every existing import (`from app.models import User, News, Subscription, ...`) still resolves unchanged because all legacy ORM classes live in the new `__init__.py`. Confirmed by importing `tests.conftest` and the full set of existing ORM models (User/News/Subscription/Meta/CompanyDataCache/ApiKey/ApiUsage/AuditLog/StripeEvent). The package layout now cleanly hosts both ORM models (in `__init__.py`) and Pydantic schemas (in sibling modules — starting with `company.py` here).

**Files:**
- `migrations/versions/014_add_companies_table.py` — new Alembic revision (revision='014', down_revision='013'). Creates `companies` with 14 spec columns + `delisted_at TIMESTAMP NULL`. PK on `ticker`, UNIQUE on `cik`, plain indexes on `sector` and `industry`. For `idx_companies_active` uses a dialect gate: Postgres gets `postgresql_where=sa.text('delisted_at IS NULL')` (partial index); any other dialect (SQLite for local tests) gets a plain index on `ticker`. This keeps the migration portable. Downgrade drops indexes then table.
- `app/models/__init__.py` — converted from the former `models.py` file. Added SQLAlchemy `Company` ORM class after `CompanyDataCache`, imported additional SA types (Date, DateTime, func). Added `idx_companies_sector` and `idx_companies_industry` in `__table_args__`; intentionally did NOT declare `idx_companies_active` as an ORM index so `Base.metadata.create_all` (used by SQLite test bootstraps) emits no partial-index DDL — the Alembic migration is the source of truth against real Postgres.
- `app/models/company.py` — new Pydantic v2 schema. Fields mirror the schema exactly: `ticker: str`, `name: str` required; everything else Optional with appropriate types (`Optional[date]` for ipo_date, `Optional[datetime]` for created_at/updated_at/delisted_at). `is_active: bool = True` default. `model_config = {"from_attributes": True}` so `Company.model_validate(orm_row)` works — future repositories (US-009) will use this to hand Pydantic models to callers.
- `migrations/env.py` — appended `Company` to the noqa F401 import block so `target_metadata` includes the new table (defensive; the migration uses imperative `op.create_table` and doesn't rely on metadata).

**Dialect gate rationale:** Postgres-specific `postgresql_where` clauses fail on SQLite with a cryptic syntax error. Per OQ-5 the partial index is only useful when millions of delisted rows sit alongside active ones; for local dev/tests the plain index is functionally equivalent. Gate idiom: `if op.get_bind().dialect.name == 'postgresql': ... else: ...`.

**Self-validation:**
1. `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/python -c 'from app.models import User, News, Subscription, Meta, CompanyDataCache, ApiKey, ApiUsage, AuditLog, StripeEvent, Company'` → OK, all 10 ORM classes importable.
2. `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/python -c 'from app.models.company import Company as PydC; p = PydC(ticker="AAPL", name="Apple Inc.", delisted_at=None); print(p.ticker, p.is_active)'` → `AAPL True`.
3. Ran Postgres 16 container on port 5433 (to dodge host system-postgres on 5432), `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://signal:signal@localhost:5433/signal_news alembic upgrade head` → ran 001 through 014 cleanly.
4. `\d companies` in Postgres shows all 17 columns (14 spec + is_active + created_at + updated_at + delisted_at; `created_at`/`updated_at` have `DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP`, `is_active` defaults TRUE), and all 5 indexes incl. `idx_companies_active btree (ticker) WHERE delisted_at IS NULL`.
5. `SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='companies'` → pk_companies, uq_companies_cik, idx_companies_sector, idx_companies_industry, idx_companies_active.
6. `alembic downgrade -1` → clean; subsequent `\d companies` errors with 'Did not find any relation'; `alembic upgrade head` round-trips back to 014.
7. `INSERT INTO companies(ticker, name) VALUES ('AAPL','Apple Inc.')` → `is_active=t`, `created_at` auto-populated — server defaults fire correctly.
8. `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/company.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file. (Unscoped `mypy app/models/company.py` exits 1 only because of pre-existing errors in `app/services/{cache_manager,edgar_client,market_data}.py` that were flagged during US-001 — none are in this story's new code.)

**Environment notes for Tester:**
- **Host has a system Postgres on 127.0.0.1:5432.** Bringing up `signal-postgres` via `docker compose --profile prod up -d postgres` succeeds but doesn't publish a host port (canonical `docker-compose.yml` has no `ports:` mapping for postgres) — so `localhost:5432` resolves to SYSTEM postgres where user/db don't exist, and `alembic upgrade` will fail with 'password authentication failed for user signal'. Two options:
  (a) Stop system postgres first: `sudo systemctl stop postgresql` then `docker compose --profile prod up -d postgres` uses port 5432 for the container... except the compose file doesn't publish ports either. So this won't work.
  (b) Run a one-off container with an explicit published port, which is what I used: `docker run -d --name signal-postgres-test -p 5433:5432 -e POSTGRES_USER=signal -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=signal -e POSTGRES_DB=signal_news postgres:16-alpine`, wait until `docker exec signal-postgres-test pg_isready -U signal` succeeds, then `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://signal:signal@localhost:5433/signal_news alembic upgrade head`. Tear down with `docker stop signal-postgres-test && docker rm signal-postgres-test`.
  (c) Or exec into the container for psql: `docker exec signal-postgres-test psql -U signal -d signal_news -c '\d companies'`.
- For mypy: `/home/haotiant/conda/bin/mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/company.py`. The scoped flag matches US-001 precedent for dealing with pre-existing errors in modules transitively pulled via `app.database`.
- For Pydantic: project is on `pydantic==2.12.5`; `model_config = {'from_attributes': True}` is the v2 equivalent of v1's `Config.orm_mode = True`.
- `app/models/__init__.py` *is* the former `app/models.py` — git rename detection should show it as `RM app/models.py -> app/models/__init__.py` plus a content diff with the new Company class + new SA imports (Date, DateTime, func).
- Downgrade test: after `alembic downgrade -1`, the `companies` table is gone; re-run `alembic upgrade head` to restore before handing off to downstream stories (US-003 depends on companies existing for its FK).
userStories.test_evidence: See jojo/test_results/US-002/attempt-1/ — validate.sh (full reproduction script), upgrade.log (alembic 001→014 clean), companies_schema.log (\d companies: all 14 spec columns + is_active + created_at + updated_at + delisted_at = 17 total; 5 indexes incl. partial idx_companies_active ON companies(ticker) WHERE delisted_at IS NULL), indexes.log (lists idx_companies_sector, idx_companies_industry, idx_companies_active), orm_import.log (stdout: AAPL), pyd_import.log (stdout: AAPL), downgrade.log (clean downgrade 014→013), post_downgrade.log ('Did not find any relation named "companies"'), mypy.log ('Success: no issues found in 1 source file').
userStories.test_report: ALL 6 assertions PASSED against commit cecca8b. Environment: one-off Postgres 16-alpine container on host:5433 → container:5432 (per implementor-documented workaround for host system-postgres collision on :5432). (1) `alembic upgrade head` ran migrations 001→014 with no errors, final line 'Running upgrade 013 -> 014, Add companies master reference table'; `\d companies` lists all 14 spec columns (ticker, cik, name, exchange, sector, industry, country, currency, description, website, employee_count, founded_year, ipo_date, is_active) plus created_at, updated_at, and nullable delisted_at (17 cols total), with 5 indexes including partial idx_companies_active btree (ticker) WHERE delisted_at IS NULL. (2) `SELECT indexname FROM pg_indexes WHERE tablename='companies'` returned 5 rows including the three required: idx_companies_sector, idx_companies_industry, idx_companies_active. (3) `python3 -c "from app.models import Company; c = Company(ticker='AAPL', name='Apple Inc'); print(c.ticker)"` exited 0 with stdout 'AAPL'. (4) `python3 -c "from app.models.company import Company as P; p = P(ticker='AAPL', name='Apple Inc.', delisted_at=None); print(p.ticker)"` exited 0 with stdout 'AAPL'. (5) `alembic downgrade -1` exited 0 with no errors, final line 'Running downgrade 014 -> 013'; subsequent `\d companies` produced 'Did not find any relation named "companies"' confirming the table was dropped. (6) `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/company.py` returned 'Success: no issues found in 1 source file' (scoped flag per US-001 precedent, same reason: pre-existing errors in app/services/* are unrelated to this story). Zero new type errors introduced. Artifacts at jojo/test_results/US-002/attempt-1/.

userStories.title: Migration — company_financials (append-only seasonal)
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a company_financials table that is append-only and keyed by (ticker, period_end, period_type) so historical filings are preserved for backtests.
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 015_add_company_financials.py creates company_financials per spec §1 Layer 2
Composite primary key (ticker, period_end, period_type) enforced
Foreign key ticker → companies(ticker) with ON DELETE RESTRICT
Index idx_financials_period on (ticker, period_end DESC)
app/models/financials.py adds Pydantic Financials model
app/models.py adds SQLAlchemy CompanyFinancials model
Migration is reversible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Append-only by composite PK — re-ingestion of same period is rejected by PK violation, eliminating dedup logic.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; \d company_financials shows all expected columns

Insert smoke test: psql -c "INSERT INTO companies(ticker, name) VALUES ('AAPL','Apple'); INSERT INTO company_financials(ticker, period_end, period_type, fiscal_year, revenue) VALUES ('AAPL','2025-12-31','Q4',2025,90000000000)" exits 0
Duplicate PK rejection: second identical insert returns SQL error
python3 -c "from app.models.financials import Financials; Financials(ticker='AAPL', period_end='2025-12-31', period_type='Q4', fiscal_year=2025)" exits 0
mypy app/models/financials.py exits 0
userStories.implementation_notes:
Added Alembic revision 015 creating company_financials (append-only seasonal) with composite PK (ticker, period_end, period_type), FK ticker → companies(ticker) ON DELETE RESTRICT, and idx_financials_period on (ticker, period_end DESC).

Files:
- migrations/versions/015_add_company_financials.py: revision='015', down_revision='014'. Creates company_financials with all 22 columns from the spec (4 PK/metadata + 6 income stmt + 5 balance sheet + 4 cash flow + 3 metadata). Money fields are BIGINT, ratio/per-share fields are NUMERIC(10,4). server_default CURRENT_TIMESTAMP on ingested_at. Index uses sa.text() with 'period_end DESC' so DESC order is preserved on Postgres (SQLite tolerates but doesn't use).
- app/models/financials.py: new Pydantic v2 Financials with from_attributes=True. Composite PK fields required, all metric fields Optional. Uses Decimal for Numeric columns, int for BigInteger.
- app/models/__init__.py: imported BigInteger, Numeric, ForeignKeyConstraint, PrimaryKeyConstraint from sqlalchemy and added CompanyFinancials ORM class matching the migration. No secondary index declared on the ORM class (Alembic is source-of-truth for DESC index DDL).
- migrations/env.py: appended CompanyFinancials to the noqa F401 import list for metadata registration.

Self-validation:
- AST parse OK on all files.
- `from app.models.financials import Financials; Financials(ticker='AAPL', period_end='2025-12-31', period_type='Q4', fiscal_year=2025)` — the ISO date string coerces via Pydantic v2.
- Alembic upgrade head on Postgres 16 (one-off container on :5433 per US-002 pattern): ran 001→015 cleanly; `\d company_financials` shows 22 columns, PK (ticker, period_end, period_type), FK to companies ON DELETE RESTRICT, idx_financials_period btree (ticker, period_end DESC).
- Duplicate-PK rejection: second identical INSERT produces `duplicate key value violates unique constraint "pk_company_financials"`.
- `alembic downgrade -1` → 015→014 clean; `\d company_financials` errors with 'Did not find any relation'; `alembic upgrade head` round-trips back to 015.
- `mypy --follow-imports=silent app/models/financials.py` → Success: no issues found in 1 source file.

Environment notes for Tester:
- Use the US-002 one-off Postgres pattern: `docker run -d --name signal-postgres-test -p 5433:5432 -e POSTGRES_USER=signal -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=signal -e POSTGRES_DB=signal_news postgres:16-alpine`, wait for `pg_isready`, then `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://signal:signal@localhost:5433/signal_news alembic upgrade head`. Host system-postgres on :5432 lacks the `signal` role; binding :5433 dodges the collision.
- INSERT test requires a companies row first (FK): `INSERT INTO companies(ticker, name) VALUES ('AAPL','Apple');` before inserting into company_financials.
- Use `--follow-imports=silent` on the mypy command to scope out pre-existing errors in app/services/* (US-001 precedent).

userStories.title: Migration — company_fundamentals + company_fundamentals_history (SCD-2)
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a current-view company_fundamentals table plus an immutable company_fundamentals_history table so callers can fetch the latest fundamentals fast and also retrieve point-in-time snapshots.
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 016_add_company_fundamentals.py creates company_fundamentals (PK = ticker) per spec §1 Layer 2 with all forward-looking metrics
Creates company_fundamentals_history with columns from spec PLUS valid_from TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, valid_to TIMESTAMP NOT NULL, PK (ticker, valid_from), index (ticker, valid_to DESC)
Postgres trigger fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update: BEFORE UPDATE on company_fundamentals, copy OLD row to company_fundamentals_history with valid_from = OLD.updated_at, valid_to = NOW()
Foreign key ticker → companies(ticker) on both tables
app/models/fundamentals.py adds Pydantic Fundamentals and FundamentalsHistory models
Migration reversible (drops trigger first)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Type-2 SCD via Postgres trigger — application code never has to remember to snapshot. History queries hit the small history table; current reads stay fast.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; both tables and trigger exist (\d company_fundamentals, \d company_fundamentals_history, psql -c "SELECT tgname FROM pg_trigger WHERE tgname='fn_snapshot_fundamentals_before_update'" returns 1 row)

SCD test script: insert AAPL row with pe_ratio=30, then UPDATE to pe_ratio=32 — psql -c "SELECT count(*) FROM company_fundamentals_history WHERE ticker='AAPL'" returns 1, and the historical row has pe_ratio=30
python3 -c "from app.models.fundamentals import Fundamentals; Fundamentals(ticker='AAPL', market_cap=3000000000000)" exits 0
mypy app/models/fundamentals.py exits 0

userStories.title: Migration — company_competitors
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a company_competitors table to store the directional similarity graph between tickers.
acceptanceCriteria: New Alembic revision 017_add_company_competitors.py creates the table per spec §1 Layer 3
Composite PK (ticker, competitor_ticker), CHECK constraint ticker != competitor_ticker
Index idx_competitors_score on (ticker, similarity_score DESC)
app/models/competitors.py adds Pydantic Competitor model
Migration reversible
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-4 resolution: competitors sourced from Polygon get_related_companies; embedding-based builder deferred to a follow-up PRD. Records source='polygon' in inserted rows.
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; \d company_competitors matches spec
Self-reference rejection: INSERT INTO company_competitors VALUES ('AAPL','AAPL',0.5,'manual',NOW()) returns CHECK violation

userStories.title: BaseRepository with cache-aside pattern + cache_keys module
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a BaseRepository class that encapsulates the cache-aside pattern (Redis read → DB read → Redis populate) so all six repos share consistent behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: app/repositories/base.py defines BaseRepository[T] generic over Pydantic model type
Method cached_get(key, ttl, db_loader) checks Redis, falls back to DB loader, populates Redis on miss
Method cached_get_list(key, ttl, db_loader) same pattern for lists
Method invalidate(key) removes a cache key
Cache-miss exceptions on Redis are caught and logged; DB loader still runs (Redis is non-critical)
app/cache/cache_keys.py exports key builders: company_master, company_fundamentals, company_financials_latest, company_competitors_top, company_institutions_top, company_insiders_recent
All keys follow company:{ticker}:{domain} namespacing per spec §2
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for US-009 and US-010. Designed so future async swap is a mechanical def→async def conversion (no Session leakage, primitives in/out, Pydantic models returned).
test_assertions: python3 -c "from app.cache.cache_keys import company_master; assert company_master('AAPL') == 'company:AAPL:master'" exits 0

pytest tests/repositories/test_base.py -v exits 0 with all tests passing, covering: (a) cache hit returns cached value without calling db_loader, (b) cache miss calls db_loader and populates Redis, (c) Redis exception falls through to db_loader
mypy app/repositories/base.py app/cache/cache_keys.py exits 0

userStories.title: CompanyRepository (master table)
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a CompanyRepository that reads/writes the companies master table with cache-aside.
acceptanceCriteria: app/repositories/company_repo.py defines CompanyRepository with: get(ticker) cache-aside via BaseRepository.cached_get, 24h TTL per spec §2
upsert(company) — INSERT ... ON CONFLICT (ticker) DO UPDATE; invalidates cache key on success
list_by_sector(sector) — DB-only (low value to cache)
Returns Pydantic models, not SQLAlchemy rows
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: First domain repo — establishes the pattern for the other 5 (US-010).
test_assertions: pytest tests/repositories/test_company_repo.py -v exits 0 covering: (a) upsert then get returns same data, (b) second get is a cache hit, (c) upsert invalidates cache so subsequent get reflects update
python3 -c "from app.repositories.company_repo import CompanyRepository; r = CompanyRepository(); print(type(r).__name__)" exits 0
mypy app/repositories/company_repo.py exits 0

userStories.title: Remaining 5 repositories (financials, fundamentals, competitors, institutions, insiders)
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want repositories for the remaining 5 domains, each following the BaseRepository pattern with TTLs from spec §2.
acceptanceCriteria: app/repositories/financials_repo.py: get_latest(ticker), get_range(ticker, from, to), append(financials) — 1h TTL on latest
app/repositories/fundamentals_repo.py: get(ticker), get_at(ticker, ts) (queries history table for SCD-2), upsert(fundamentals) — 5min TTL on current; trigger handles history snapshot on UPDATE
app/repositories/competitors_repo.py: get_top(ticker, n), upsert_batch(ticker, competitors) — 24h TTL
app/repositories/institutions_repo.py: get_top(ticker, n, as_of=None), append_batch(holders) — 6h TTL on top-N
app/repositories/insiders_repo.py: get_recent(ticker, days), append(txn) (idempotent via UNIQUE) — 15min TTL
All return Pydantic models
Each repo has a unit test file under tests/repositories/
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Five repos in one story — each is small (~50-80 LoC) and follows the US-009 pattern. SCD-2 read path proven via get_at() returning historical row.
test_assertions: pytest tests/repositories/ -v exits 0; each repo's tests cover get/upsert(or append)/cache invalidation
Fundamentals SCD test: insert v1, update to v2, call get_at(ticker, v1_timestamp) returns v1 data
python3 -c "from app.repositories import financials_repo, fundamentals_repo, competitors_repo, institutions_repo, insiders_repo; print('ok')" exits 0
mypy app/repositories/ exits 0

userStories.title: CompanyService.get_full_profile() with parallel fan-out + on-demand backfill
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a service-layer aggregator that fetches all 6 domains in parallel via ThreadPoolExecutor, returns a single CompanyProfile, and synchronously backfills missing domains from upstream (Polygon/EDGAR) on cache+DB miss, guarded by a per-(ticker, domain) Redis mutex to prevent thundering herd.
acceptanceCriteria: app/services/company_service.py defines CompanyService.get_full_profile(ticker) -> CompanyProfile
Uses concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=6) to parallelize the 6 repo calls
Each repo call wrapped in try/except — failure of one domain does not fail the whole profile (returns None for that field, sets partial=True flag)

On-demand backfill: when a repo call returns None (cache + DB miss), service attempts synchronous fetch from upstream client (Polygon for fundamentals/competitors/financials, EDGAR for institutions/insiders), persists via upsert/append, returns freshly-fetched data

Per-(ticker, domain) mutex via Redis SETNX with 30s TTL: key format lock:company:{ticker}:{domain}. If lock held, wait up to 5s polling for the lock holder's result; if still empty, return None + partial=True
Mutex always released in finally block (even on exception)
app/models/company_profile.py defines Pydantic CompanyProfile aggregate (company, fundamentals, latest_financials, competitors, top_institutions, recent_insiders, partial: bool, fetched_at: datetime)
On total cache hit (all 6 keys present), latency <50ms p99 measured locally
On cold ticker (full backfill needed), latency <5s p99 (bounded by upstream API + mutex wait)
Repository interfaces written so future async migration is mechanical (return Pydantic models, no Session leakage, methods could be async def with same signature)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-3 resolution: Option B (synchronous on-demand backfill) with Redis mutex to prevent thundering-herd duplicate upstream fetches. Cold ticker self-warms; subsequent calls hit cache.
test_assertions: pytest tests/services/test_company_service.py -v exits 0 covering: (a) all 6 fields populated when data exists in cache, (b) graceful degradation when one repo raises and upstream also fails (partial=True), (c) parallel execution: mock each repo with 100ms sleep — total time <200ms, (d) on-demand backfill: with empty DB, mock upstream to return AAPL competitors → service returns competitors AND psql -c "SELECT count(*) FROM company_competitors WHERE ticker='AAPL'" returns ≥1 after the call, (e) mutex behavior: spawn 5 concurrent threads calling get_full_profile('XYZ') against empty cache+DB, mock upstream with 1s sleep — assert upstream called exactly once

python3 -c "from app.services.company_service import CompanyService; from app.models.company_profile import CompanyProfile; print(CompanyProfile.model_fields.keys())" exits 0 and includes all 6 domain fields plus partial and fetched_at
mypy app/services/company_service.py app/models/company_profile.py exits 0

userStories.title: GET /api/company/<ticker>/profile route
userStories.description: As a frontend engineer, I want a single endpoint that returns the full company profile so the Company panel doesn't need to compose 6 separate calls.
acceptanceCriteria: New blueprint app/routes/company.py exposes GET /api/company/<ticker>/profile
Calls CompanyService.get_full_profile(ticker) and returns its JSON
Returns 404 if companies table has no row for ticker AND no on-demand fetch succeeds
Same auth/tier-gating decorators as existing /api/market/<symbol>/details route
Blueprint registered in app/__init__.py
Response includes Cache-Control: private, max-age=60 header
Typecheck passes
Browser verification: open Company panel for AAPL on /terminal, confirm panel renders with data from new endpoint (DevTools Network tab shows /api/company/AAPL/profile call)
userStories.notes: First user-facing wire-up. Existing /api/market/<symbol>/* routes still serve the terminal panel (US-013 will rewire them).
test_assertions: curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/company/AAPL/profile -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq '.company.ticker' outputs "AAPL"
Response JSON has top-level keys: company, fundamentals, latest_financials, competitors, top_institutions, recent_insiders, partial, fetched_at
404 path: curl -sw "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8000/api/company/ZZZZZ/profile outputs 404
Playwright: navigate to /terminal as a Max user, click an AAPL ticker badge, verify Company panel mounts and Network shows /api/company/AAPL/profile 200
mypy app/routes/company.py exits 0

userStories.title: Refactor existing /api/market/<symbol>/* routes to use new repositories
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want the 6 existing /api/market/<symbol>/* routes to read from the new normalized tables (via repositories) instead of company_data_cache JSON, so we have a single source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: app/routes/market.py routes (/details, /financials, /competitors, /institutions, /insiders) refactored to call the corresponding repository
On cache + DB miss, route triggers an on-demand Polygon/EDGAR fetch, persists result via repo's upsert/append, then returns
app/services/cache_manager.py CompanyCache class marked @deprecated; existing company_data_cache table is dual-written (old class continues to fire) for the soak period
Response shape backward compatible — existing terminal panels keep working without frontend changes
Typecheck passes
Browser verification: existing Company panel still works (Fundamentals, Financials, Competitors, Institutions, Insiders tabs all render)
userStories.notes: Backward-compat is critical — 7-day soak begins from this story's deploy date before US-018 can drop the old table. Snapshot diff response shapes pre/post to catch regressions.
test_assertions: curl -s http://localhost:8000/api/market/AAPL/details -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq '.ticker' outputs "AAPL" and shape matches pre-refactor response (snapshot diff)
All 5 routes (/details, /financials, /competitors, /institutions, /insiders) return 200 with non-empty payloads for AAPL
Playwright: navigate to /terminal, click AAPL ticker badge, click through all 5 tabs, verify each renders without errors
DB verification: after a route hit for a previously-unknown ticker, psql -c "SELECT count(*) FROM companies WHERE ticker='<new_ticker>'" returns 1
mypy app/routes/market.py exits 0

userStories.title: Backfill script — migrate company_data_cache JSON to normalized tables
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a one-shot CLI script that reads existing company_data_cache rows and writes them into the new normalized tables, so we don't lose the cache we've already paid to populate.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/backfill_company_data.py reads all company_data_cache rows, parses JSON data column by data_type, writes to appropriate normalized table via the new repositories
Idempotent — re-running on same data is a no-op (UPSERT semantics)
--dry-run flag prints planned writes without committing
--data-type flag scopes to one type (details, financials, etc.) for incremental backfill
Logs progress every 100 rows; reports final counts (succeeded, failed, skipped) per data_type
Failed parses do not abort the run — logged to jojo/test_results/US-014/attempt-N/failures.log
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Must run AFTER US-009 and US-010 so repos exist. Pre-condition for US-018 (drop deprecated table) — verify zero data loss before deprecating.
test_assertions: python3 scripts/backfill_company_data.py --dry-run exits 0 and prints planned write counts
After actual run on a populated company_data_cache: psql -c "SELECT count(*) FROM companies" ≥ count of unique symbols in cache with data_type='details'
Idempotency: running twice in a row → second run reports 0 new inserts
python3 scripts/backfill_company_data.py --data-type=financials only touches company_financials table
mypy scripts/backfill_company_data.py exits 0

userStories.title: Scheduled ingestion — EDGAR pollers (10-Q/K, 13F window-aware, Form 4) + monthly SEC calendar probe
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want APScheduler jobs that periodically poll SEC EDGAR for new filings and write them to the normalized tables, so company data refreshes without depending on user requests. 13F polling uses hardcoded filing windows (Feb/May/Aug/Nov, derived from SEC 45-day deadline), with a monthly SEC calendar probe that overrides the hardcoded date if SEC publishes a different deadline.
acceptanceCriteria: app/ingestion/edgar_ingester.py defines ingest_10q_10k(tickers), ingest_13f(tickers), ingest_form4(tickers) — all idempotent via PK/UNIQUE

APScheduler jobs in app/worker.py: edgar_10q_10k (daily 02:00 UTC), edgar_form4 (every 30 min), edgar_13f_baseline (daily 03:00 UTC), edgar_13f_intensive (hourly, no-op outside active window), edgar_13f_calendar_probe (00:05 UTC on the 1st of Feb/May/Aug/Nov)

edgar_13f_calendar_probe queries SEC for the official 13F deadline; persists (quarter, deadline_date) to ingestion_calendar table or Redis key edgar:13f:deadline:{YYYY-Q}; falls back to hardcoded if call fails
app/ingestion/edgar_calendar.py — pure function get_active_13f_window(now) reads persisted deadline if present, otherwise computes from hardcoded rule; returns 14-day window or None
Job scope: tickers in companies table where delisted_at IS NULL
Each job invalidates corresponding Redis cache keys after writing
EDGAR rate limit (10 req/sec) respected via existing throttle in app/services/edgar_client.py:35-36
Job failures logged via app/services/audit.py with event_type='ingestion_failure'
Calendar probe failures logged but do not crash scheduler — fallback to hardcoded windows
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-2 resolution: hardcoded windows + monthly probe gives accuracy without per-run external calls. 13F intensive job is hourly but no-ops outside the 14-day window — cheap when idle.
test_assertions: python3 -c "from app.ingestion.edgar_ingester import ingest_10q_10k; ingest_10q_10k(['AAPL'])" exits 0; psql -c "SELECT count(*) FROM company_financials WHERE ticker='AAPL'" ≥ 1
Idempotency: run twice; row count unchanged after second run
python3 -c "from app.worker import scheduler; print([j.id for j in scheduler.get_jobs()])" includes edgar_10q_10k, edgar_13f_baseline, edgar_13f_intensive, edgar_13f_calendar_probe, edgar_form4

Calendar window logic: python3 -c "from app.ingestion.edgar_calendar import get_active_13f_window; from datetime import datetime; print(get_active_13f_window(datetime(2026,2,10)))" returns a date tuple; get_active_13f_window(datetime(2026,3,10)) returns None
Calendar probe override: pre-populate Redis edgar:13f:deadline:2026-Q1 with a date 7 days from "now", call get_active_13f_window(now) → returns the override-derived window, not the hardcoded one
Form 4 ingest: python3 -c "from app.ingestion.edgar_ingester import ingest_form4; ingest_form4(['AAPL'])" exits 0 and at least one row in insider_transactions for AAPL
After ingest, Redis keys company:AAPL:financials:latest and company:AAPL:insiders:30d are absent or refreshed (verify by setting sentinel before, asserting deleted/replaced)
mypy app/ingestion/edgar_ingester.py app/ingestion/edgar_calendar.py exits 0

userStories.title: Scheduled ingestion — Polygon fundamentals refresh
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want a scheduled job that refreshes company_fundamentals (market_cap, PE, beta, etc.) from Polygon for all tracked tickers.
acceptanceCriteria: app/ingestion/market_data_ingester.py defines refresh_fundamentals(tickers)
Calls PolygonClient.get_ticker_details() per ticker, maps fields to company_fundamentals row, calls fundamentals_repo.upsert() (which fires the SCD-2 trigger)
APScheduler job polygon_fundamentals runs every 15 minutes during US market hours (9:30–16:00 ET, weekdays), hourly outside
Job scope: tickers in companies table where is_active=TRUE AND delisted_at IS NULL
Redis key company:{ticker}:fundamentals invalidated after each upsert
Polygon rate limit respected (5 req/sec on free tier — use existing throttle if present, else add)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Trigger from US-004 fires automatically on every UPDATE — no app code needed for SCD-2 snapshot.
test_assertions: python3 -c "from app.ingestion.market_data_ingester import refresh_fundamentals; refresh_fundamentals(['AAPL'])" exits 0; psql -c "SELECT market_cap FROM company_fundamentals WHERE ticker='AAPL'" returns a non-null bigint
Second run updates updated_at AND creates a new row in company_fundamentals_history (proves SCD-2 trigger fires)
Scheduler registration: python3 -c "from app.worker import scheduler; assert 'polygon_fundamentals' in [j.id for j in scheduler.get_jobs()]" exits 0
mypy app/ingestion/market_data_ingester.py exits 0

userStories.title: S&P 500 initial seed + scheduled refresh for core tickers
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want to seed the companies table with the S&P 500 constituents up front (rate-gated so we don't blow EDGAR's 10 req/sec limit), and add a scheduled job that keeps these core tickers fresh on a tighter cadence than organic-growth tickers.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/seed_sp500.py — one-shot CLI that reads S&P 500 tickers from data/sp500_tickers.txt, runs full ingest sequence per ticker (companies UPSERT, ingest_10q_10k, refresh_fundamentals, ingest_13f, ingest_form4, competitors fetch via Polygon get_related_companies)
Rate-gated to ≤8 req/sec to EDGAR and ≤4 req/sec to Polygon via token-bucket rate limiter (not naive sleep())
Resumable — tracks progress in seed_progress table or data/seed_progress.json so re-running after a crash skips already-completed tickers; --reset flag wipes progress
--dry-run flag lists tickers that would be processed without making API calls
Logs per-ticker outcome (ok/partial/failed) and a final summary
data/sp500_tickers.txt checked into the repo with current S&P 500 constituents
APScheduler job core_ticker_refresh in app/worker.py: every 4 hours, calls refresh_fundamentals + ingest_form4 for all S&P 500 tickers (10-Q/13F continue via US-015 jobs)
Organic growth path (US-011 on-demand backfill) unchanged and serves any non-S&P 500 ticker — no special-casing in CompanyService
Audit log entry written when seed completes: event_type='sp500_seed_complete' with row counts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OQ-1 resolution: hybrid seed + organic. Estimated runtime ~7-10 min for full S&P 500 seed. Long-tail tickers handled by US-011 mutex-guarded on-demand backfill.
test_assertions: python3 scripts/seed_sp500.py --dry-run exits 0 and prints ≥500 ticker symbols
After actual run on a fresh DB: psql -c "SELECT count(*) FROM companies" ≥ 500; psql -c "SELECT count(DISTINCT ticker) FROM company_fundamentals" ≥ 400
Resumability: kill the script mid-run after ~50 tickers, re-run without --reset → output reports 'skipping N already-processed tickers' and resumes
--reset: re-run with --reset → starts from ticker #1 again
Rate limit: instrument with a counter — observed EDGAR request rate stays ≤8 req/sec across the full run (assert via log analysis in jojo/test_results/US-017/attempt-N/rate.log)
Scheduler registration: python3 -c "from app.worker import scheduler; assert 'core_ticker_refresh' in [j.id for j in scheduler.get_jobs()]" exits 0
wc -l data/sp500_tickers.txt outputs a count between 500 and 510
mypy scripts/seed_sp500.py exits 0

userStories.title: Drop deprecated company_data_cache table (after 7-day soak)
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I want to remove the deprecated company_data_cache table and CompanyCache class once we've verified the new system is stable for 7 days, so we don't carry dead weight.
acceptanceCriteria: Pre-condition gate: implementation_notes confirms (a) US-013 deployed ≥7 days ago, (b) zero company_data_cache reads in last 7 days (verified via audit log or grep on production logs), (c) all 5 market routes serving from new tables
New Alembic revision 020_drop_company_data_cache.py drops the table
app/services/cache_manager.py CompanyCache class deleted
All call sites of CompanyCache removed (should be zero by this point)
Migration reversible (re-creates empty table on downgrade)
Typecheck passes
Browser verification: terminal Company panel still works after deploy
userStories.notes: Final cleanup. Block on the 7-day soak gate — do NOT drop early even if everything looks fine. Soak protects against rare edge cases (e.g., cron job that touches the table once a week).
test_assertions: alembic upgrade head exits 0; psql -c "\d company_data_cache" errors with "did not find any relation"
grep -r "CompanyCache" app/ scripts/ --include="*.py" returns zero results (excluding the migration itself)
Playwright: open Company panel for AAPL on /terminal, verify all 5 tabs render
All existing tests pass: pytest tests/ -v exits 0
mypy app/ exits 0
